D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Spherical vs Parabolic Mirror

toggle sphere or parabola and see how peripheral rays miss the paraxial focus

A spherical mirror focuses only paraxial rays cleanly; peripheral rays converge short of F, producing a caustic blur. A paraboloid focuses every parallel ray to F exactly.

Toggle sphere ↔ parabola and slide R. The crimson reflected rays show the caustic in spherical mode; in parabolic mode they all meet at F.
